title: Add `--dry-run` flag to `podman [volume,image,system] prune`
body:
### Feature request description

Cleaning up is difficult if you don't want to use prune (it has the disadvantage that you can't control was is being deleted). 

### Suggest potential solution

For cleaning up manually, a list of all unused resources would be highly valuable. 


### Have you considered any alternatives?

Or give prune a dry-run option and make it explicity list the resources that would be removed.

comments.body: I think you need to elaborate on your request here.  What's a "resource' to you?
I just need volumes and images. But for broader use, it would be great to have the resouces that you can delete with prune.

@danishprakash 

prune does not have a dry-run function. If it had, it would solve the problem.
A dry run sounds like a good idea. Let's see what @podman-maintainers think about it?
Hey I would like to give it a try, I would like to start from `podman image prune`
